paths.title: Learn ASL
paths.tagline: Bite-sized units across every part of daily ASL.

units.title: Getting Started
units.description: Your first signs, greetings, and introductions.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
When someone's signing to you, watch their face — eyebrows and expression tell you a lot.
Pair each sign with its word.
Pair each sign with its word.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Let your face match what you're signing — a happy sign needs a happy face.
Saying no? Shake your head as you sign — your face and hands work together.
Pair each sign with its word.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Skip mouthing the English word under your sign — your mouth is part of the sign.
Pair each sign with its word.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Keep your hands off your mouth while signing so people can read your expression.
Keep signs in front of your chest — not down in your lap or up by your chin.
Stick with one signing hand — switching mid-sign makes you harder to follow.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Slow and clean beats fast and sloppy — speed comes once the sign feels natural.
Pair each sign with its word.
Record yourself and watch it back — you'll spot things you miss in the mirror.
Pair each sign with its word.

units.title: Getting Help
units.description: Ask for help, clarity, and safety.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Same handshape, different spot on your body — often a completely different word.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Fill the blank in the sentence.
Fingerspell in a steady rhythm — smooth flow beats racing through letters.
Keep a relaxed hand — tension makes letters blur together.

units.title: Feelings & Emotions
units.description: How you feel and express personality.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Pair each sign with its word.
Double letters? Slide or bounce slightly instead of holding the same shape twice.
Pair each sign with its word.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Keep fingerspelling near shoulder height in a small area — don't let it drift down.
Yes/no question? Raise your eyebrows. Who, what, or where? Lower them.
Pair each sign with its word.
Place each person in a spot in space — then point back there for he, she, or they.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Quoting someone? Turn toward them, sign their words, then turn back.
Signs like give and tell move toward whoever receives them — aim the action.
Pair each sign with its word.

units.title: Deaf Culture
units.description: ASL identity and the Deaf community.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Words like today and tomorrow usually come first in the sentence.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Done with your thought? Hold the last sign, then lower your hands.
Pair each sign with its word.
Need someone's attention? Wave, tap their shoulder, or flick the lights.

units.title: First Conversations Checkpoint
units.description: Show what you remember from First Conversations (Getting Started, Everyday Replies, You & Me, Getting Help, Feelings & Emotions, Meet People, Deaf Culture).

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.

units.title: Everyday Actions

steps.prompt: One person signs at a time — wait for a pause before you jump in.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.

Walked through two people signing? A quick sorry smooths it over.
Pair each sign with its word.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
Set the coffee down — two free hands show the full sign.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
